microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized, self-service system sustaining 45+ annual resilience drills and driving **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise partners including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and enabled scalable partner-led execution across distributed engineering teams.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering a full drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** and surfacing critical hardware defects that established a new continuous-validation reference architecture.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a **sovereign-cloud network isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract requiring precision coordination across security and infrastructure teams.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, restructuring execution cycles to reduce planning time by 39% and increase drill throughput capacity by 21% without adding headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows, conducting hands-on demos and enablement sessions that drove active utilization across 14 Azure service teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly** in data pipeline authoring tasks.

Shaped the product roadmap by facilitating **11+ structured user interviews** with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps that directly influenced prioritization of intent-based YAML generation and OTel-aligned telemetry tooling.

Improved data integrity and retrieval by enforcing metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** platform, cutting average lookup time by 83% across internal engineering and partner-facing workflows.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ service teams and complex multi-org data pipeline dependencies.

Drove discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual toil per region launch**, then built a roadmap to automate critical paths — directly mirroring the partner enablement and self-sufficiency model Cribl applies to its VAR ecosystem.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to prioritize high-impact automation investments and measure progress against region-launch velocity targets in real time.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit OS migration pilot by defining the legacy transition strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating cross-functional delivery across IT, Operations, and Engineering.

Implemented automated **CI/CD pipelines** in collaboration with Engineering and IT, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable release architecture across distributed robotics infrastructure.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, resolving **40+ high-priority backlog tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and maintain delivery velocity during a high-stakes platform transition.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end CRM workflow for 200+ monthly proposals, standardizing the scoping and invoicing lifecycle to reduce cycle time and improve close-rate consistency across sales motions.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy that optimized SEO, Google Business presence, and targeted outreach channels.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify margin leaks, delivering data-backed recommendations that tightened cost structures and improved per-job contribution margins.
